sections.paragraph:
Λούκιος, ου, ὁ (Diod S 11, 81, 1; 12, 43, 1; 14, 38, 1; SIG 1173, 7; Sb II 258f; PFay 110, 1 [94 a.d.]; PWarr 1, 1; PVindobBosw 2, 11; Jos., Bell. 6, 188; Just., A I, 1, 1 [Verus, adopted son of Antonius]; A II 2, 15 Λούκιός τις al.; Ath. ins [Commodus]; B-D-F §41, 1; Mlt-H. 88) Lucius.

① of Cyrene, a teacher or prophet at Antioch Ac 13:1.—HCadbury, Beginn. I 5, ’33, 489–95.

② an otherw. unknown Christian, sender of a greeting Ro 16:21.—On both s. Λουκάς.—M-M.

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

PWarr PWarr = The Warren Papyri—List 4

PVindobBosw PVindobBosw = Einige Wiener Papyri, ed. EBoswinkel—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

Cyrene Cyrene = text fr. Cyrene

Beginn. Beginn. = The Beginnings of Christianity, ed. FJFoakesJackson and KLake—List 6

otherw. otherw. = otherwise

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 603
